items.title: NVIDIA backs Amkor with $1.5bn to expand AI packaging in the US
items.why: Nvidia commits $1.5B prepayment to Amkor to expand advanced packaging capacity in the US, signaling a strategic push to diversify CoWoS-class packaging supply outside TSMC.

items.title: Intel Second-Quarter Revenue Rises 25% to $16.1 Billion
items.why: Intel Q2 revenue rose 25% YoY to $16.1B—fastest growth since 2011—driven by AI datacenter demand, with Q3 guidance also beating estimates.

items.title: As AI grows more powerful, a US-China feud threatens safety efforts - Reuters
items.why: Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan targets the $100B custom AI chip market and backs a $20B+ capex push, framing Intel Foundry as central to next-gen AI silicon supply.

items.title: China Seeks US Clarity on AI Talks as Sanction Threats Loom - Bloomberg.com
items.why: AMD Helios AI server rack enters production using Samsung HBM4, confirming Samsung as a qualified HBM4 supplier for a major GPU rack platform.

items.title: TGV still faces technical barriers; CoPoS commercialization not likely until 2030 - digitimes
items.why: Digitimes reports CoPoS (chip-on-panel-on-substrate) still faces TGV technical barriers and won't commercialize until 2030, pushing out a key next-gen packaging timeline.

items.title: ASML beats Q2 earnings on AI chip demand, raises 2026 revenue forecast up to 38% growth - Pluang
items.why: ASML beats Q2 earnings on AI chip demand and raises its 2026 revenue forecast to up to 38% growth, validating continued EUV-driven capex cycle strength.

items.title: TRUMPF Korea: "Strong Demand for HBM and Automation to Drive Double-Digit Revenue Growth"
items.why: TRUMPF Korea forecasts double-digit revenue growth for FY2026-27 specifically citing strong HBM demand, a useful secondary confirming signal on laser/equipment supply tightening.

items.title: TEMC CNS Resumes SK hynix Semiconductor Materials Shipments After Fire Recovery
items.why: TEMC CNS resumes TiCl₄ shipments to SK Hynix after a 10-month fire-related outage, removing a thin-film deposition materials risk for Hynix's HBM production lines.

items.title: DI Second-Quarter Operating Profit Seen Hitting Record, Up 170% From Year Earlier
items.why: DI (semiconductor test equipment) expected to report Q2 operating profit up 170% YoY, reflecting surging test demand tied to HBM and advanced packaging ramp.

items.title: AMD to supply Anthropic with 2 gigawatts of Instinct MI450 GPUs — will invest up to $5 billion in the Claude developer, which is already using MI355X GPUs
items.why: AMD will supply Anthropic with up to 2 GW of MI450 GPUs in Helios rack-scale systems, with first 1 GW online H1 2027, plus a $5B investment—a landmark scale-out commitment for non-Nvidia AI infrastructure.

items.title: AMD to Supply Helios AI Systems to Microsoft in Second Half
items.why: AMD's Helios rack-scale AI systems will begin shipping to Microsoft Azure in H2 2026, marking a significant AMD foothold in hyperscaler inference infrastructure alongside the Anthropic deal.

items.title: Wistron Opens $700M Texas Plant to Build NVIDIA GB300 and Vera Rubin AI Systems
items.why: Wistron's $700M Fort Worth plant begins volume production of NVIDIA GB300 Grace Blackwell Ultra and Vera Rubin systems, establishing a key U.S. onshore AI server manufacturing node for NVIDIA.

items.title: Engineered for Agentic AI: NVIDIA HGX B300 on CoreWeave Cloud
items.why: CoreWeave announces availability of NVIDIA HGX B300 at scale, extending the Blackwell GPU family to its cloud platform ahead of broader neocloud competition.

items.title: CoreWeave Becomes One of the First Cloud Providers to Achieve NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud Validation for Inference on NVIDIA GB200 NVL72
items.why: CoreWeave achieves NVIDIA Exemplar Cloud validation for both training and inference on GB200 NVL72, a certification benchmark that matters for enterprise procurement decisions.

items.title: Fortinet becomes Intel 4's first foundry customer, following firewall ASIC deal — CEO Lip-Bu Tan's promised foundry wins begin to surface, but on a mature node
items.why: Fortinet's SP6 security processor becomes Intel 4's first external foundry win, a small but symbolically important proof point for Lip-Bu Tan's foundry turnaround strategy.

items.title: White House Official Says Moonshot Accessed Banned Nvidia Chips
items.why: A White House official alleges China's Moonshot used banned Nvidia chips to build Kimi K3, escalating enforcement scrutiny around export controls on advanced AI accelerators.

items.title: Adata chairman says DRAM shortage will last another 10 years — dismisses AI bubble talk until '2040 or 2050'
items.why: ADATA's chairman predicts a decade-long DRAM shortage driven by AI demand, framing memory as one of the world's two scarcest resources alongside green power through 2035+.

items.title: Soitec Reports First Quarter Revenue of Fiscal Year 2027
items.why: Soitec's Q1 FY2027 revenue report is a primary earnings signal for the SOI wafer supply chain underpinning RF, power, and photonics semiconductors relevant to AI infrastructure.

items.title: OpenAI’s AI spending spree has ballooned to $750B
items.why: OpenAI's infrastructure spend through 2030 has grown to $750B—equivalent to Sweden's GDP—underscoring the capex backdrop driving demand for accelerators, memory, power, and optical interconnects.

items.title: MGX, AIP, and BlackRock’s GIP complete $40bn acquisition of Aligned Data Centers
items.why: The $40B MGX/BlackRock GIP acquisition of Aligned Data Centers closes as the largest-ever data center deal, signaling continued massive capital deployment into AI-ready infrastructure.

items.title: AMD and Anthropic Announce Strategic Partnership to Deploy Up to 2 Gigawatts of AMD Instinct MI450 Series GPUs
items.why: AMD and Anthropic announce a strategic partnership to deploy up to 2 GW of MI450-series GPUs, a major supply commitment signaling AMD's growing share of hyperscale AI training against Nvidia.

items.title: Meta to use custom AMD Instinct MI400 accelerators with 144GB of HBM4 for select workloads, report claims — could dramatically reduce cost at the expense of versatility
items.why: Meta reportedly using a custom MI400 with 144 GB HBM4 (reduced from standard config) to cut cost for specific inference workloads, a concrete HBM4 deployment data point and AMD customization signal.

items.title: OpenAI Plans to Spend Over $30 Billion on Georgia Data Center
items.why: OpenAI commits over $30 billion to a single Georgia data center campus near Savannah, the largest announced AI infrastructure investment to date.

items.title: Daeduck Electronics Pursues Multiple LTAs With Global Tech Giants
items.why: Daeduck Electronics is negotiating multiple long-term supply agreements with global tech giants for semiconductor substrates, signaling tightening advanced packaging substrate supply.

items.title: Electrically-pumped near-infrared VCSEL epitaxially-grown on Si
items.why: arXiv paper demonstrates electrically-pumped near-infrared VCSEL epitaxially grown directly on Si, addressing a critical bottleneck for scalable silicon photonics integration.

items.title: Lightwave Logic looks to augment silicon photonics in the race for 400G optics
items.why: Lightwave Logic outlines its polymer modulator approach to complement silicon photonics at 400G, relevant to CPO/LPO transceiver roadmaps.

items.why: Reuters Breakingviews on Zhongji Innolight's ~$7B HK IPO pricing (up to HK$1,010/share, July 30 listing) highlights Chinese optical transceiver supplier accessing public capital at scale.

items.title: Broadcom Solution Demo: 128G PAM-4 SerDes for PCIe Gen7 Applications - Broadcom
items.why: Broadcom demos 128G PAM-4 SerDes for PCIe Gen7, directly relevant to scale-up interconnect bandwidth and switch silicon roadmaps.

items.why: Samsung reportedly building hybrid bonding mass production line with full-scale deployment targeted 2029–2030, signaling a packaging roadmap shift with HBM and chiplet implications.

items.title: CEA-Leti Looks Beyond SRAM and DRAM as AI Reshapes the Memory Roadmap
items.why: CEA-Leti's François Andrieu outlines embedded, persistent, and low-energy memory architectures beyond SRAM/DRAM to meet AI inference demands.

items.title: SK Hynix Officially Denies Intel Ohio Factory Acquisition Rumors as "Groundless" - finance.biggo.com
items.why: SK Hynix officially denies Intel Ohio fab acquisition, closing a supply-chain speculation loop relevant to HBM capacity outlook.

items.title: Microsoft to spend billions on GPUs to be shared with AI lab Mistral

items.title: Z.ai powers up a 1-gigawatt AI data center built entirely on Chinese chips, report claims - MSN

items.title: Crusoe to deploy 5GW of ON.energy's AI UPS systems across data center portfolio
items.why: Crusoe contracts 5 GW of AI-optimized UPS from ON.energy, a significant power infrastructure commitment signaling scale of next-gen AI datacenter buildouts.

items.why: First components of ASML's $400M lithography tool arriving in Albany marks a concrete step in US advanced semiconductor manufacturing capacity expansion.

items.title: TE Connectivity delivers results above guidance with 14% sales growth and 19% EPS growth in third quarter of fiscal 2026
items.why: TE Connectivity reports record Q3 sales with 14% growth and guides another double-digit quarter, reflecting robust demand for connectors in AI datacenter and optical interconnect applications.

items.title: Google reportedly developing 'Frozen v2' chip with Gemini's architecture etched into the silicon — engineers project 6 to 10 times more tokens per watt than latest TPUs
items.why: Google's 'Frozen v2' chip reportedly hardcodes Gemini architecture into silicon, projecting 6–10× better tokens-per-watt than current TPUs—a major custom silicon efficiency claim worth tracking.

items.title: Wistron Celebrates Grand Opening of First U.S. Smart Factory Marking Milestone in Global Smart Manufacturing Strategy
items.why: Wistron's $700M Fort Worth factory is now producing NVIDIA GB300 Grace Blackwell Ultra Superchips in the US, marking the first domestic mass production of that platform.

items.why: SK hynix details CTI (Channel Topology Innovation) enabling 176-layer 3D NAND toward mass production, a primary-source technical explainer on their AI-NAND roadmap.

items.why: Dell'Oro forecasts the high-end router and aggregation switch market reaching $19B by 2030, with AI infrastructure as the primary growth driver—key market sizing data point.

items.why: China's Z.ai reportedly operating a 1GW data center on domestic chips, the largest known AI compute cluster built without Nvidia hardware—significant geopolitical signal.

items.why: Earnings preview identifying Alphabet, Intel, and networking/semiconductor names reporting this week—useful framing for what AI infrastructure metrics Wall Street is watching.

items.why: ADTRAN preliminary Q2 2026 results are a primary earnings signal for the optical networking and transceiver supply chain ahead of full results.

items.why: Astera Labs launches industry-first 3.2Tbps Smart Retimers/Redrivers supporting 200G-per-lane Ethernet, UALink, and ESUN for next-gen AI interconnects.

items.why: Super Micro preliminary results show record backlog exceeding $60B in new orders, signaling sustained AI server infrastructure demand.

items.why: CoreWeave details its first-to-bring-up status for NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72, with specifics on infrastructure innovations enabling the new platform.

items.why: SMIC's N+3 achieves TSMC N6-equivalent transistor density without EUV, marking a significant Chinese process node advance with geopolitical supply chain implications.

items.why: Kimi K3's 2.8T open-weight model undercuts US rivals on price but demands extreme hardware scale, shaping AI infrastructure procurement and neocloud demand.
